member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.ConverterCache
member.summary: Tracks all inputs to converter construction and caches the results of construction itself.
member.param: An immutable configuration that this cache builds upon.

remarks.para: This type is observably immutable and thread-safe.

This type offers something of an information barrier to converter construction. The only gets a reference to this object, and this object does not have a reference to . This ensures that properties on cannot serve as inputs to the converters. Thus, the only properties that should reset the are those declared on this type.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Converters.ArraysOfPrimitivesConverters
member.summary: Contains a bunch of converters for arrays of primitives.
member.remarks: These aren't strictly necessary, but because we can predict their max encoded representation and embed the direct reader/writer calls, we can avoid the overhead of many tiny calls to and , which speeds things up considerably.

member.summary: The factor by which the input values span length should be multiplied to get the minimum output buffer length.
member.remarks: Booleans are unique in that they always take exactly one byte. All other primitives are assumed to take a max of their own full memory size + a single byte for the msgpack header.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Converters.PrimitiveConverterLookup
member.summary: Provides access to built-in converters for primitive types.
member.remarks: This class is carefully crafted to help trimming be effective by avoiding type references to types that are not used in the application. Although the retrieval method references all the the fact that it is generic gives the JIT/AOT compiler the opportunity to only reference types that match the type argument (at least for the value types).

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Converters.PrimitivesAsDynamicConverter
member.summary: A converter that can write msgpack primitives or other convertible values typed as and reads everything into primitives, dictionaries and arrays.

remarks.para: This converter is not included by default because untyped serialization is not generally desirable. But it is offered as a converter that may be added to in order to enable limited untyped serialization.
This converter is very similar to . What distinguishes this class is that the result can be used with the C# dynamic keyword to index into maps using string keys as if they were properties.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Converters.PrimitivesAsDynamicConverter.Instance
member.summary: Gets the default instance of the converter.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Converters.PrimitivesAsObjectConverter
member.summary: A converter that can write msgpack primitives or other convertible values typed as and reads everything into primitives, dictionaries and arrays.

remarks.para: This converter is not included by default because untyped serialization is not generally desirable. It is offered as a converter that may be added to in order to enable limited untyped serialization.

Maps are deserialized as objects that implement where the key is and the value is a nullable . This converter deserializes an entire msgpack structure using these primitives, since no type information is available to deserialize any sub-graph as a higher-level type.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Converters.PrimitivesAsObjectConverter.Instance
member.summary: Gets the default instance of the converter.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Converters.PrimitivesAsObjectConverter.Read(Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackReader@,Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializationContext)
member.summary: Reads any one msgpack structure.
member.param: The msgpack reader.
The serialization context.
member.returns: A , , , , , , , with keys and values, an array of s, or an .
member.exception: Thrown if a msgpack map includes a key.

remarks.para: Maps implement with keys and values.

Keep in mind when using integers that msgpack doesn't preserve integer length information. This method deserializes all integers as (or if the value is negative). Integer length stretching is automatically applied when using integers as keys in maps so that they can match.
All arrays are instances of object?[].
Msgpack binary data is represented as a byte[] object.
Msgpack extensions are represented by values.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Converters.RawMessagePackConverter
member.summary: Provides the raw msgpack transport intended for the type.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Converters.RawMessagePackConverter.Read(Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackReader@,Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializationContext)
member.remarks: We always copy the msgpack into another buffer from the buffer we're reading from because we don't know how long that will last. For async deserialization, the buffer literally may not even last through the end of deserialization. And async deserialization may invoke this (synchronous) deserializing method as an optimization, so we really have no idea whether this buffer will last till the user has a chance to read from it.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.DeserializeDefaultValuesPolicy
member.summary: Enumerates policies that control how deserialization handles default values.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.DeserializeDefaultValuesPolicy.Default
member.summary: Deserialization will fail if any required properties have no specified values in the data stream, or when values are encountered for non-nullable properties.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.DeserializeDefaultValuesPolicy.AllowNullValuesForNonNullableProperties
member.summary: Allow a value to be deserialized into a non-nullable property.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.DeserializeDefaultValuesPolicy.AllowMissingValuesForRequiredProperties
member.summary: Allows deserialization to succeed even if required properties are not specified in the data stream. These will be left at their default values.
member.remarks: This flag includes , since accepting missing values will tend to leave reference typed properties at their default values.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.DynamicMsgPackDictionary
member.summary: A dictionary of untyped, deserialized objects and values.

remarks.para: This dictionary may be used with the C# dynamic keyword to expose its members as properties as well as through its indexer. For example, if the dictionary contains a key "Foo", then d.Foo will return the value associated with that key as well as d["Foo"]. Non-string keys are not supported as properties, but they can still be accessed through the indexer.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.DynamicMsgPackDictionary.#ctor(System.Collections.Generic.IReadOnlyDictionary{System.Object,System.Object})
member.summary: A dictionary of untyped, deserialized objects and values.

For example, if the dictionary contains a key "Foo", then d.Foo will return the value associated with that key as well as d["Foo"]. Non-string keys are not supported as properties, but they can still be accessed through the indexer.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.IStructuralSecureEqualityComparer`1.GetHashCode
member.summary: Gets a hash code for this object, which may not be collision resistant.
member.returns: A 32-bit integer.

remarks.para: This method should be compatible with such that if two objects are structurally equal, they will return the same hash code. On types that implement with shallow equality, this means this method may need to be implemented as an explicit interface implementation so it can have structural equality semantics while can match the default shallow equality semantics.
On .NET, the default implementation of this method is to truncate the result of .

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.KeyAttribute
member.summary: Specifies an ordinal key that may be used when the object serializes its properties for a faster and/or more compact binary representation.

remarks.para: Once this key is applied to any field or property of a type, it must be applied to all fields and properties of that type that are candidates for serialization. Reassigning a key to a different member of the same type is an unversioned breaking change in the serialization schema and should be avoided when reading or writing across versions is supported. Unassigned indexes in the array will be left as nil during serialization and ignored during deserialization.

An object that uses this attribute may be serialized as an array of values where the index given to this attribute becomes the index into the array for the value of the applied property, or the object may be serialized as a map where the index given to this attribute provides the key instead of the property name, and the map value is set to the value of the property. Whether an object serializes as a map or an array is determined at runtime. When is not and there are "holes" in the would-be array (due to properties with default values or unused indexes), the map format will be chosen when a quick estimate determines that it will be a more compact representation than an array with holes in it. Deserializers should always be prepared for either the map or the array representation of the object.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.KeyAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The index into the array where the value of the annotated member will be stored.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.KeyAttribute.Index
member.summary: Gets the array index where the value of the annotated member will be stored.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackConverter`1
member.summary: An base class for all MessagePack converters.
member.typeparam: The data type that can be converted by this object.

remarks.para: Authors of derived types should review this documentation for important guidance on implementing a converter.

Key points to remember about each or method (or their async equivalents):
Read or write exactly one msgpack structure. Use an array or map header for multiple values.
Call before any significant work.
Delegate serialization of sub-values to a converter obtained using rather than making a top-level call back to .
Implementations are encouraged to override in order to support .

member.summary: Serializes an instance of .
member.param: The writer to use.
The value to serialize.
Context for the serialization.
member.returns: A task that tracks the async serialization.

remarks.para: The default implementation delegates to and then flushes the data to the pipe if the buffers are getting relatively full.

Derived classes should only override this method if they may write a lot of data. They should do so with the intent of writing fragments of data at a time and periodically call
in order to keep the size of memory buffers from growing too much.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackConverter`1.ReadAsync(Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackAsyncReader,Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializationContext)
member.summary: Deserializes an instance of .
member.param: The reader to use.
Context for the deserialization.
member.returns: The deserialized value.

remarks.para: The default implementation delegates to after ensuring there is sufficient buffer to read the next structure.

Derived classes should only override this method if they may read a lot of data. They should do so with the intent to be able to read some data then asynchronously wait for data before reading more in order to reduce the amount of memory required to buffer.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackConverter`1.GetJsonSchema(Nerdbank.MessagePack.JsonSchemaContext,PolyType.Abstractions.ITypeShape)
member.summary: Gets the JSON schema that resembles the data structure that this converter can serialize and deserialize.
member.param: A means to obtain schema fragments for inclusion when your converter delegates to other converters.
The shape of the type , in case it provides useful metadata for constructing the schema.
member.returns: The fragment of JSON schema that describes the value written by this converter, or if this method has not been overridden.

remarks.para: Implementations should return a new instance of that represents the JSON schema fragment for every caller. A shared instance may be used to call and the result returned.
The type property should not include "null" on account of the converted type being a reference type. It is the consumer of the object that will determine whether is an acceptable value of the object.
Custom converters that do not override this method will lead to a JSON schema that does not describe the written data, and allows any data as input.
If the converter delegates to other converters, the schemas for those sub-values can be obtained for inclusion in the returned schema by calling on the .

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackPrimitives
member.summary: Primitive msgpack encoding/decoding methods.
Primitive msgpack encoding/decoding methods.
Primitive msgpack encoding/decoding methods.
member.devremarks: This class makes aggressive use of `ref byte`-based APIs rather than the simpler and safer span-indexing based APIs. The reason for this is it makes an appreciable reduction in assembly code size and improved performance. We don't give up safety because we always check the buffer length ourselves first. See this sample code to see how much smaller the code is by following these patterns.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackPrimitives.DecodeResult
member.summary: Enumerates the possible outcomes of a read operation.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackPrimitives.DecodeResult.Success
member.summary: The token was successfully read from the buffer.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackPrimitives.DecodeResult.TokenMismatch
member.summary: The token read from the buffer did not match the expected token.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackPrimitives.DecodeResult.EmptyBuffer
member.summary: The buffer is empty and no token could be read.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackPrimitives.DecodeResult.InsufficientBuffer
member.summary: The token is of the expected type, but the buffer does not include all the bytes needed to read the value.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackPrimitives.Decoders
member.summary: Contains jump tables that translate a msgpack byte directly to a method that reads an integer of the corresponding type.
member.remarks: These jump tables are *very* fast. They cost only an array lookup (virtually free) and a virtual method call (quite cheap). It is faster than a switch statement because it avoids the need to compare the byte to each possible value or range. It is also faster than using delegates, because the delegate call is more expensive than a virtual method call.

member.param: The buffer to write to. This should be at least 1 byte in length to ensure success.
The number of bytes required to write the value, whether successful or not.
member.returns: if was large enough and the value written; otherwise, . When , the value of is indicates how many bytes are required to write the value successfully.

member.returns: if was large enough and the array header written; otherwise, . When , the value of indicates how many bytes are required to write the array header successfully.
member.remarks: Writes the length of the next array to be written in the most compact form of , , or .

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackPrimitives.TryWrite(System.Span{System.Byte},System.SByte,System.Int32@)
member.summary: Writes a signed integer value to the specified buffer, if the buffer is large enough.
member.param: The buffer to write to. This should be at least 2 bytes in length to ensure success with any value for .
The signed integer value to write.
The number of bytes required to write the value, whether successful or not.
member.returns: if was large enough and the value written; otherwise, . When , the value of indicates how many bytes are required to write the value successfully.
member.remarks: The smallest possible representation is used for the value, which may be as few as 1 byte. In addition to the built-in 1-byte code when within specific MessagePack-supported ranges, any of the following encodings may also be used: , .

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer
member.summary: Serializes .NET objects using the MessagePack format.

remarks.para: This type is immutable and thread-safe.
Changes to properties on this object should be performed with the record syntax keyword.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer.LibraryExtensionTypeCodes
member.summary: Gets the extension type codes to use for library-reserved extension types.
member.remarks: This property may be used to reassign the extension type codes for library-provided extension types in order to avoid conflicts with other libraries the application is using.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer.StartingContext
member.summary: Gets the starting context to begin (de)serializations with.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer.MaxAsyncBuffer
member.summary: Gets the maximum length of msgpack to buffer before beginning deserialization.
member.value: May be set to any non-negative integer. The default value is 1MB and is subject to change.

remarks.para: Larger values are more likely to lead to async buffering followed by synchronous deserialization, which is significantly faster than async deserialization. Smaller values are useful for limiting memory usage since deserialization can happen while pulling in more msgpack bytes, allowing release of the buffers containing earlier bytes to make room for subsequent ones.

This value has no impact once deserialization has begun. The msgpack structure to be deserialized and converters used during deserialization may result in buffering any amount of msgpack beyond this value.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer.DisableHardwareAcceleration
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether hardware accelerated converters should be avoided.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer.DeserializeDynamicPrimitives(Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackReader@,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Deserializes msgpack into primitive values, maps and arrays, where the maps are specially implemented to play nicely with the C# dynamic keyword.
member.param: The source of msgpack to decode.
A cancellation token.

remarks.para: The resulting object is designed to work conveniently with the C# dynamic type. Maps become dynamic objects whose members are named after the keys in the maps. When maps do not use string keys, the values are still accessible by using the indexer on the object. Attempts to access values with keys that do not exist on the original msgpack object result in throwing a . All members on maps are discoverable using , which enumerates the keys on the object.

example.para: The following snippet demonstrates a way to use this method.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackStreamingReader
member.summary: A msgpack decoder that returns error codes rather than throws exceptions when the buffer is incomplete or the token type does not match expectations.
member.remarks: All decoding methods on this struct return . Callers must take care to observe this value and take appropriate action. A common calling pattern is to call the decoding method within a loop's expression and use the extension method on the result. The content of the loop should be a call to and to reconstruct the reader using .

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackStreamingReader.GetMoreBytesAsync
member.summary: A delegate that can be used to get more bytes to complete the operation.
member.param: A state object.

The position after the last consumed byte (i.e. the last byte from the original buffer that is not expected to be included to the new buffer). Any bytes at or following this position that were in the original buffer must be included to the buffer returned from this method.
The position of the last examined byte. This should be passed to when applicable to ensure that the request to get more bytes is filled with actual more bytes rather than the existing buffer.
A cancellation token.
member.returns: The available buffer, which must contain more bytes than remained after if there are any more bytes to be had.

member.remarks: The reader position is changed when the return value is . The reader position and the may also be changed when the return value is , such that after fetching more bytes, a follow-up call to this method can resume skipping.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackStreamingReader.GetExchangeInfo
member.summary: Gets the information to return from an async method that has been using this reader so that the caller knows how to resume reading.
member.returns: The value to pass to .

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackStreamingReader.FetchMoreBytesAsync(System.UInt32)
member.summary: Adds more bytes to the buffer being decoded, if they are available.
member.param: The minimum number of bytes to fetch before returning.
member.returns: The value to pass to .
member.exception: Thrown if no more bytes are available.
member.remarks: This is a destructive operation to this value. It must not be used after calling this method. Instead, the result can use the result of this method to recreate a new value.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackStreamingReader.TryReadBinHeader(System.UInt32@)
member.summary: Tries to read the header of binary data.
member.param: Receives the length of the binary data, when successful.
member.returns: The result classification of the read operation.
member.remarks: A successful call should always be followed by a successful call to , with the length specified by (even if zero), so that the overall structure can be recorded as read.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackStreamingReader.TryReadStringHeader(System.UInt32@)
member.summary: Tries to read the header of a string.
member.param: Receives the length of the next string, when successful.
member.returns: The result classification of the read operation.
member.remarks: A successful call should always be followed by a successful call to , with the length of bytes specified by the extension (even if zero), so that the overall structure can be recorded as read.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackStreamingReader.ReadStringSlow(System.UInt32,System.String@)
member.summary: Reads a string assuming that it is spread across multiple spans in the .
member.param: The length of the string to be decoded, in bytes.
Receives the decoded string.
member.returns: The result of the operation.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackStreamingReader.Advance(System.Int64,System.UInt32,System.UInt32)
member.summary: Advances the reader past the specified number of bytes.
member.param: The number of bytes to advance.
The number of msgpack structures that has been read. Typically 1, sometimes 0.
The number of msgpack structures added to the expected count. Typically 0, but for array/map headers will be non-zero.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackString
member.summary: A .NET string together with its msgpack encoding parts, to optimize serialization of well-known, often seen strings such as property names used in a msgpack map object.
member.remarks: This class is not a substitute for string interning. Activate string interning via the property. This class avoids the need to encode/decode strings, which is even more efficient than interning strings, but it is meant only for a finite sized set of well-known strings.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackString.#ctor(System.String)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the class.
member.param: The string to pre-encode for msgpack serialization.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackString.Value
member.summary: Gets the string value behind this instance.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackString.Utf8
member.summary: Gets the UTF-8 encoded bytes of the string.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackString.MsgPack
member.summary: Gets the msgpack encoded bytes of the string.
member.value: The msgpack encoded bytes are the UTF-8 encoded bytes of the string, prefixed with the msgpack encoding of the string length.
member.remarks: The value of this property is suitable for providing to the method.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackString.IsMatch(System.ReadOnlySpan{System.Byte})
member.summary: Checks whether a given UTF-8 encoded string matches the string in .
member.param: The UTF-8 encoded string to test against.
member.returns: if the strings match; otherwise .
member.remarks: This method is allocation free.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackString.TryRead(Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackReader@)
member.summary: Checks the string at the current reader position for equality with this string.
member.param: The reader. The reader's position will be advanced if and only if the next msgpack token is a matching string.
member.returns: if the string matched; otherwise.

remarks.para: No exception is thrown if the reader is positioned at a non-string token.
This method never allocates memory nor encodes/decodes strings.

member.exception: Thrown if the reader has no more tokens, or the buffer contains an incomplete string token.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters
member.summary: Contains extension methods to add optional converters.
member.remarks: The library comes with many converters. Some are not enabled by default to avoid unnecessary dependencies and to keep a trimmed application size small when it doesn't require them. The extension methods in this class can be used to turn these optional converters on.

member.summary: The msgpack format used to store values.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.GuidFormat.StringN
member.summary: The will be stored as a string in the msgpack stream using the "N" format.
member.remarks: An example of this format is "69b942342c9e468b9bae77df7a288e45".

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.GuidFormat.StringD
member.summary: The will be stored as a string in the msgpack stream using the "D" format, which is the default format used by .
member.remarks: An example of this format is "69b94234-2c9e-468b-9bae-77df7a288e45".

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.GuidFormat.StringB
member.summary: The will be stored as a string in the msgpack stream using the "B" format.
member.remarks: An example of this format is "{69b94234-2c9e-468b-9bae-77df7a288e45}".

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.GuidFormat.StringP
member.summary: The will be stored as a string in the msgpack stream using the "P" format.
member.remarks: An example of this format is "(69b94234-2c9e-468b-9bae-77df7a288e45)".

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.GuidFormat.StringX
member.summary: The will be stored as a string in the msgpack stream using the "X" format.
member.remarks: An example of this format is "{0x69b94234,0x2c9e,0x468b,{0x9b,0xae,0x77,0xdf,0x7a,0x28,0x8e,0x45}}".

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.GuidFormat.BinaryLittleEndian
member.summary: The will be stored in a compact 16 byte binary representation, in little endian order.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.WithAssumedDateTimeKind(Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer,System.DateTimeKind)
member.summary: Configures the built-in converter to assume a specific when serializing values that have a kind.
member.param: The serializer to add converters to.
Either or .
member.returns: The modified serializer.
member.remarks: By default, serializing a with an kind throws an exception because the MessagePack format does not permit that kind of ambiguity. While an explicit is always preferred, this method allows you to assume a specific for such values. Such assumptions will be recorded in the serialized data, such that deserializing the data will produce a with the specified in .
member.exception: Thrown if this method has already been called on the given or a custom converter has already been set.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.WithExpandoObjectConverter(Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer)
member.summary: Adds a converter for to the specified serializer.
member.param: The serializer to add converters to.
member.returns: The modified serializer.
member.exception: Thrown if a converter for has already been added.

remarks.para: This can only serialize an whose properties are values or objects whose runtime type has a shape available as provided by .

This can deserialize any msgpack map. Nested maps included in the deserialized graph will be deserialized as dictionaries that support C# dynamic access to their members, similar to but with read-only access.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.WithObjectConverter(Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer)
member.summary: Adds a converter to the specified serializer that can write objects with a declared type of based on their runtime type (provided a type shape is available for the runtime type), and can deserialize them based on their msgpack token types into primitives, dictionaries and arrays.
member.param: The serializer to add converters to.
member.returns: The modified serializer.
member.exception: Thrown if a converter for has already been added.
member.remarks: Deserialized arrays will be typed as arrays. Deserialized dictionaries will be typed with keys and values.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.OptionalConverters.WithDynamicObjectConverter(Nerdbank.MessagePack.MessagePackSerializer)
member.summary: Adds a converter to the specified serializer that can write objects with a declared type of based on their runtime type (provided a type shape is available for the runtime type), and can deserialize them based on their msgpack token types into primitives, dictionaries and arrays.
member.param: The serializer to add converters to.
member.returns: The modified serializer.
member.exception: Thrown if a converter for has already been added.
member.remarks: This converter is very similar to the one added by , except that the deserialized result can be used with the C# dynamic keyword where the content of maps can also be accessed using keys as if they were properties.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.RawMessagePack
member.summary: Represents a sequence of raw msgpack bytes.

remarks.para: This struct is useful as the type for some field or property that should be serialize or deserialized separately from its surrounding data. For example an RPC protocol may have an envelope around user data, such that the envelope and the user data should have distinct serialization rules. The envelope could use this in order to facilitate this by allowing pre-serialization and deferred deserialization of user data.
The for this type will always copy the memory from the buffers being read so that this struct has an independent lifetime.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.RawMessagePack.ToOwned
member.summary: Produces a self-sustaining copy of this struct that will outlive whatever the original source buffer was from which this was created.
member.returns: A copy of the data that is guaranteed to be immutable. When is already , will be returned instead of making a redundant copy.

remarks.para: This is useful when its owner wants the data to live longer than the underlying buffers from which it was created.

This struct mutates itself as well to retain possession of the cloned data, so that multiple calls to this method do not result in redundant copies. This will only be effective when the caller stores the struct in a mutable location. If this struct is stored in a field for instance, the local copy cannot be mutated, though the returned copy will actually be self-owned.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.ReferenceEqualityTracker.ReferenceEqualityComparer
member.summary: An that explicitly disregards any chance of by-value equality or hash code computation, since we very explicitly want to preserve references without accidentally combining two distinct objects that happen to be considered equal.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.ReferencePreservationMode
member.summary: An enumeration of the modes supported for preserving references in serialized object graphs.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.ReferencePreservationMode.Off
member.summary: References are not preserved. Attempts to deserialize a msgpack stream that includes references will throw .

remarks.para: If an object appears multiple times in a serialized object graph, it will be serialized anew at each location it is referenced. This has two outcomes: redundant data leading to larger serialized payloads and the loss of reference equality when deserialized.
Serialization will throw (typically crashing the process) if the object graph contains cycles.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.ReferencePreservationMode.RejectCycles
member.summary: References are preserved. Cycles are rejected during serialization and deserialization.

remarks.para: When an object appears multiple times in an object graph, it will be serialized as a reference to the first occurrence. Deserialization will create a new object graph wherein objects are shared across the graph as they were in the original object graph. Of course there will not be reference equality between the original and deserialized objects, but the deserialized objects will have reference equality with each other.

This option utilizes a proprietary msgpack extension and can only be deserialized by libraries that understand this extension. A reference requires between 3-6 bytes in the msgpack stream instead of whatever the object's by-value representation would have required.
There is a small perf penalty for this feature, but depending on the object graph it may turn out to improve performance due to avoiding redundant serializations.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.ReferencePreservationMode.AllowCycles
member.summary: References are preserved. Cycles are allowed.

remarks.para: The remarks in apply to this mode as well.

This setting adds security implications. Deserializing untrusted data with this setting enabled may lead to a denial of service attack by allowing the data source to introduce unexpected reference cycles, leading to an object graph that causes the program to crash with a later while operating on that object graph.

Not all cycles can be deserialized. Consider the cycle of A -> B -> A. If A is first to be deserialized, then this cycle can be deserialized if and only if A has a default constructor and no or properties. In brief, only the last serialized object in a cycle is allowed to have a non-default constructor or or properties due to limitations in the C# language and the way PolyType works. Since the order of serialized objects in a cycle is difficult to control in practice, any types that may be involved in cycles should avoid non-default constructors and or properties.

Derived types may override this method to return comparers where desired.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SecureHash.CollisionResistantHasherUnmanaged`1
member.summary: Provides a default implementation of for blittable structs.
member.typeparam: The type that may be compared.
member.remarks: This implementation takes the raw byte representation of a blittable struct as the hash input. This will typically be correct behavior, but in cases where multiple unique binary data represent values that are considered equivalent (specifically where would return ), this method will not return the same hash code for those values and would violate the invariant that hash codes must be equal between two values that are themselves considered equal. An example of two equivalent values that have different binary representations are and 0. For such structs, it is important to normalize the data to be hashed first.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Sequence`1.MinimumSpanLength
member.summary: Gets or sets the minimum length for any array allocated as a segment in the sequence. Any non-positive value allows the pool to determine the length of the array.

remarks.para: Each time or is called, previously allocated memory is used if it is large enough to satisfy the length demand. If new memory must be allocated, the argument to one of these methods typically dictate the length of array to allocate. When the caller uses very small values (just enough for its immediate need) but the high level scenario can predict that a large amount of memory will be ultimately required, it can be advisable to set this property to a value such that just a few larger arrays are allocated instead of many small ones.
The in use may itself have a minimum array length as well, in which case the higher of the two minimums dictate the minimum array size that will be allocated.
If is , this value may be automatically increased as the length of a sequence grows.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.Sequence`1.AutoIncreaseMinimumSpanLength
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the should be intelligently increased as the length of the sequence grows.
member.remarks: This can help prevent long sequences made up of many very small arrays.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializationContext
member.summary: Context that flows through the serialization process.

example.para: The default values on this struct may be changed and the modified struct applied to in order to serialize with the updated settings.
To modify the starting context on an existing serializer, you can use the with keyword to create a new serializer with the updated context.

member.value: 0 when no skip operation was suspended and is still incomplete.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializationContext.Item(System.Object)
member.summary: Gets or sets special state to be exposed to converters during serialization.
member.param: Any object that can act as a key in a dictionary.
member.returns: The value stored under the specified key, or if no value has been stored under that key.

remarks.para: A key-value pair is removed from the underlying dictionary by assigning a value of for a given key.

Strings can serve as convenient keys, but may collide with the same string used by another part of the data model for another purpose. Make your strings sufficiently unique to avoid collisions, or use a static readonly object MyKey = new object() field that you expose such that all interested parties can access the object for a key that is guaranteed to be unique.

member.example: To add, modify or remove a key in this state as applied to a , capture and change the as a local variable, then reassign it to the serializer.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializationContext.DepthStep
member.summary: Decrements the depth remaining and checks the cancellation token.
member.remarks: Converters that (de)serialize nested objects should invoke this once before passing the context to nested (de)serializers.
member.exception: Thrown if the depth limit has been exceeded.
Thrown if has been canceled.

member.name: M:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializationContext.ReportObjectConstructed(System.Object)
member.summary: Shares the reference to an object as soon as it is constructed during deserialization.
member.param: The constructed object.

remarks.para: This method should never be called with a boxed value type. The caller may use to determine if the value is a value type and call this method only if this property is .

Converters are not required to call this method, and there is no point in calling it just before the method returns the object to its caller. But if such a method activates the object and is about to start deserializing its properties, calling this method first can help provide support for reference cycles that involve the object being deserialized.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializeDefaultValuesPolicy
member.summary: Specifies the policy for serializing default values.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializeDefaultValuesPolicy.Never
member.summary: Do not serialize any default values.
member.remarks: Use this setting with care, because it can break round-tripping of data types that consider properties to be required. Consider setting on when using this setting.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializeDefaultValuesPolicy.Required
member.summary: Serialize default values when they are required by the schema.
member.remarks: Properties are considered required when they have the required modifier on them or they appear as parameters in the deserializing constructor without a default value specified.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializeDefaultValuesPolicy.ValueTypes
member.summary: Serialize default values for value types.
member.remarks: This means values such as 0 and will be serialized, but will not be serialized.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializeDefaultValuesPolicy.ReferenceTypes
member.summary: Serialize default values for reference types.
member.remarks: This means that properties with values will be serialized.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializeDefaultValuesPolicy.Always
member.summary: Serialize all properties, regardless of their values.

member.summary: An immutable configuration object that describes how to serialize and deserialize objects.

member.name: F:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.Default
member.summary: Gets the default configuration.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.Converters
member.summary: Gets an array of objects that should be used for their designated data types.
member.remarks: Converters in this collection are searched first when creating a converter for a given type, before and .

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.ConverterTypes
member.summary: Gets a collection of types that should be used for their designated data types.
member.remarks: The types in this collection are searched after matching and before when creating a converter for a given type.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.ConverterFactories
member.summary: Gets an array of converter factories to consult when creating a converter for a given type.
member.remarks: Factories are the last resort for creating a custom converter, coming after and .

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.DerivedTypeMappings
member.summary: Gets an array of objects that add runtime insight into what derived types may appear in the serialized data for a given base type.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.PerfOverSchemaStability
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether to boost performance using methods that may compromise the stability of the serialized schema.

remarks.para: This setting is intended for use in performance-sensitive scenarios where the serialized data will not be stored or shared with other systems, but rather is used in a single system live data such that the schema need not be stable between versions of the application.

Examples of behavioral changes that may occur when this setting is :
All objects are serialized with an array of their values instead of maps that include their property names.
Polymorphic type identifiers are always integers.

In particular, the schema is liable to change when this property is and:
Serialized members are added, removed or reordered within their declaring type.
A is removed, or inserted before the last such attribute on a given type.
Changing this property (either direction) is itself liable to alter the schema of the serialized data.

Performance and schema stability can both be achieved at once by:
Using the on all serialized properties.
Specifying explicitly for all polymorphic types.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.SerializeEnumValuesByName
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether enum values will be serialized by name rather than by their numeric value.

remarks.para: Serializing by name is a best effort. Most enums do not define a name for every possible value, and flags enums may have complicated string representations when multiple named enum elements are combined to form a value. When a simple string cannot be constructed for a given value, the numeric form is used.
When deserializing enums by name, name matching is case insensitive unless the enum type defines multiple values with names that are only distinguished by case.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.PreserveReferences
member.summary: Gets a setting that determines how references to objects are preserved during serialization and deserialization.
member.value: The default value is because it requires no msgpack extensions, is compatible with all msgpack readers, adds no security considerations and is the most performant.
member.remarks: Preserving references impacts the serialized result and can hurt interoperability if the other party is not using the same feature.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.PropertyNamingPolicy
member.summary: Gets the transformation function to apply to property names before serializing them.
member.value: The default value is null, indicating that property names should be persisted exactly as they are declared in .NET.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.InternStrings
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether to intern strings during deserialization.

remarks.para: String interning means that a string that appears multiple times (within a single deserialization or across many) in the msgpack data will be deserialized as the same instance, reducing GC pressure.
When enabled, all deserialized strings are retained with a weak reference, allowing them to be garbage collected while also being reusable for future deserializations as long as they are in memory.

This feature has a positive impact on memory usage but may have a negative impact on performance due to searching through previously deserialized strings to find a match. If your application is performance sensitive, you should measure the impact of this feature on your application.

This feature is orthogonal and complementary to . Preserving references impacts the serialized result and can hurt interoperability if the other party is not using the same feature. Preserving references also does not guarantee that equal strings will be reused because the original serialization may have had multiple string objects for the same value, so deserialization would produce the same result. Preserving references alone will never reuse strings across top-level deserialization operations either. Interning strings however, has no impact on the serialized result and is always safe to use. Interning strings will guarantee string objects are reused within and across deserialization operations so long as their values are equal. The combination of the two features will ensure the most compact msgpack, and will produce faster deserialization times than string interning alone. Combining the two features also activates special behavior to ensure that serialization only writes a string once and references that string later in that same serialization, even if the equal strings were unique objects.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.DisableHardwareAcceleration
member.summary: Gets a value indicating whether hardware accelerated converters should be avoided.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.SerializeDefaultValues
member.summary: Gets the policy concerning which properties to serialize though they are set to their default values.
member.value: The default value is , meaning that only required properties or properties with non-default values will be serialized.

remarks.para: By default, the serializer omits properties and fields that are set to their default values when serializing objects. This property can be used to override that behavior and serialize all properties and fields, regardless of their value.

Objects that are serialized as arrays (i.e. types that use on their members), have a limited ability to omit default values because the order of the elements in the array is significant. See the documentation for details.

When using anything besides , avoid using member initializers to set default values without also using . Otherwise round-trip serialization may not work as expected. For example a property declared as public int Age = 18; may be skipped during serialization when its value is 0 (because default(int) == 0), and upon deserialization, the value of Age will be 18 because no value for the property was provided. But simply adding [DefaultValue(18)] to that same property declaration allows the serializer to understand what value should be considered the default value, so that it is only skipped during serialization if the age was 18, allowing all values to be correctly round-tripped.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.DeserializeDefaultValues
member.summary: Gets the policy concerning how to handle missing or properties during deserialization.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.LibraryExtensionTypeCodes
member.summary: Gets the extension type codes to use for library-reserved extension types.
member.remarks: This property may be used to reassign the extension type codes for library-provided extension types in order to avoid conflicts with other libraries the application is using.

member.name: P:Nerdbank.MessagePack.SerializerConfiguration.ComparerProvider
member.summary: Gets the provider of and instances to use when instantiating collections that support them.
member.value: The default value is an instance of , which provides hash collision resistance for improved security when deserializing untrusted data.
member.remarks: This property may be cleared from its secure default for improved performance when deserializing trusted data.

member.name: T:Nerdbank.MessagePack.StructuralEqualityComparer
member.summary: Provides deep, by-value implementations of for arbitrary data types.

remarks.para: The deep walking of the object graph for deep by-value equality and hashing is based on the same that is used to generate MessagePack serializers. The implementation therefore considers all the same properties for equality and hashing that would be included in a serialized copy.

This implementation is not suitable for all types. Specifically, it is not suitable for types that have multiple memory representations that are considered equal. An invariant for behavior must be that if x.Equals(y) then x.GetHashCode() == y.GetHashCode(). For an auto-generated implementation of these methods for arbitrary types such as this, no specialization for multiple values that are considered equal is possible.

For example, a value has distinct memory representations for 0.0 and -0.0, yet these two values are considered equal and must have the same hash code. In this case and for several other common data types included with .NET, special consideration is built-in for correct operation. But this cannot be done automatically for any user-defined types.

When using user-defined types for which this implementation is inappropriate, a custom implementation of may be used if the type is used directly. But if the type is referenced in a type reference graph such that is used for by-value comparison, implementing on that type will allow the type to take control of just its contribution to the hash code and equality comparison. Type surrogates may alternatively be used for a simpler way to guide the comparison of a type.
Types that define no (public or opted in) properties and do not implement will throw a when attempting to create an equality comparer.
This implementation should only be used for acyclic graphs, since cyclic graphs will cause a while performing the comparison.

Another consideration is that types used as keys in collections should generally not have a changing hash code or the collections internal data structures may become corrupted by a key that is stored in the wrong hash bucket. Keys should generally be immutable to prevent this, or at least immutable in the elements that contribute to the hash code. In an automated equality comparer such as the one produced by this class, all public properties contribute to the hash code, even if they are mutable. Care should therefore be taken to not mutate properties on objects used as keys in collections.

The values are compared by their declared types rather than polymorphism. If some type has a property of type Foo, and the actual value at runtime derives from Foo, only the properties on Foo will be considered. If between two object graphs being equality checked, their runtime types do not match, the equality check will return .

member.name: M:InternedBuffers.WeakArrayCache.GetOrCreateEntry(InternedBuffers.InternableArray@,System.Boolean@)
member.summary: Main entrypoint of this cache. Tries to look up a array that matches the given internable. If it succeeds, returns the array and sets cacheHit to true. If the array is not found, calls ExpensiveConvertToArray on the internable, adds the resulting array to the cache, and returns it, setting cacheHit to false.
member.param: The internable describing the array we're looking for.
Whether the entry was already in the cache.
member.returns: A array matching the given internable.

member.summary: Frees all GC handles and clears the cache.

member.name: M:InternedBuffers.WeakArrayCache.ScavengeNoLock
member.summary: Iterates over the cache and removes unused GC handles, i.e. handles that don't reference live arrays. This is expensive so try to call such that the cost is amortized to O(1) per GetOrCreateEntry() invocation. Assumes the lock is taken by the caller.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnscopedRefAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate a byref escapes and is not scoped.

remarks.para: There are several cases where the C# compiler treats a as implicitly - where the compiler does not allow the to escape the method.

For example:
for instance methods.
parameters that refer to types.
parameters.
This attribute is used in those instances where the should be allowed to escape.

Applying this attribute, in any form, has impact on consumers of the applicable API. It is necessary for API authors to understand the lifetime implications of applying this attribute and how it may impact their users.

member.name: T:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.DynamicallyAccessedMembersAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that certain members on a specified are accessed dynamically, for example through .
member.remarks:
This allows tools to understand which members are being accessed during the execution of a program.
This attribute is valid on members whose type is or .
When this attribute is applied to a location of type , the assumption is that the string represents a fully qualified type name.
When this attribute is applied to a class, interface, or struct, the members specified can be accessed dynamically on instances returned from calling on instances of that class, interface, or struct.
If the attribute is applied to a method it's treated as a special case and it implies the attribute should be applied to the "this" parameter of the method. As such the attribute should only be used on instance methods of types assignable to System.Type (or string, but no methods will use it there).

member.summary: Gets or sets the scope of the code that is relevant for the attribute.
member.remarks: The Scope property is an optional argument that specifies the metadata scope for which the attribute is relevant.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Target
member.summary: Gets or sets a fully qualified path that represents the target of the attribute.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument identifying the analysis target of the attribute. An example value is "System.IO.Stream.ctor():System.Void". Because it is fully qualified, it can be long, particularly for targets such as parameters. The analysis tool user interface should be capable of automatically formatting the parameter.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.MessageId
member.summary: Gets or sets an optional argument expanding on exclusion criteria.
member.remarks: The property is an optional argument that specifies additional exclusion where the literal metadata target is not sufficiently precise. For example, the cannot be applied within a method, and it may be desirable to suppress a violation against a statement in the method that will give a rule violation, but not against all statements in the method.

member.name: P:System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis.UnconditionalSuppressMessageAttribute.Justification
member.summary: Gets or sets the justification for suppressing the code analysis message.

member.summary: Calculate the offset from the start using the giving collection length.
member.param: The length of the collection that the Index will be used with. length has to be a positive value
member.remarks: For performance reason, we don't validate the input length parameter and the returned offset value against negative values. we don't validate either the returned offset is greater than the input length. It is expected Index will be used with collections which always have non negative length/count. If the returned offset is negative and then used to index a collection will get out of range exception which will be same affect as the validation.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ModuleInitializerAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that a method should be called in its containing module's initializer.
member.remarks:
When one or more valid methods with this attribute are found in a compilation, the compiler will emit a module initializer which calls each of the attributed methods.
Certain requirements are imposed on any method targeted with this attribute: - The method must be `static`. - The method must be an ordinary member method, as opposed to a property accessor, constructor, local function, etc. - The method must be parameterless. - The method must return `void`. - The method must not be generic or be contained in a generic type. - The method's effective accessibility must be `internal` or `public`.
The specification for module initializers in the .NET runtime can be found here: https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/design/specs/Ecma-335-Augments.md#module-initializer

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.OverloadResolutionPriorityAttribute

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RequiredMemberAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that a type has required members or that a member is required.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.RequiresLocationAttribute
member.summary: Reserved for use by a compiler for tracking metadata. This attribute should not be used by developers in source code.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.SkipLocalsInitAttribute
member.summary: Used to indicate to the compiler that the .locals init flag should not be set in method headers.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.DisableRuntimeMarshallingAttribute
member.summary: Disables the built-in runtime managed/unmanaged marshalling subsystem for P/Invokes, Delegate types, and unmanaged function pointer invocations.
member.remarks:
The built-in marshalling subsystem has some behaviors that cannot be changed due to backward-compatibility requirements. This attribute allows disabling the built-in subsystem and instead uses the following rules for P/Invokes, Delegates, and unmanaged function pointer invocations:
- All value types that do not contain reference type fields recursively (unmanaged in C#) are blittable - Value types that recursively have any fields that have [StructLayout(LayoutKind.Auto)] are disallowed from interop. - All reference types are disallowed from usage in interop scenarios. - SetLastError support in P/Invokes is disabled. - varargs support is disabled. - LCIDConversionAttribute support is disabled.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.UnsafeAccessorAttribute
member.summary: Provides access to an inaccessible member of a specific type.

member.summary: Instantiates an providing access to a member of kind .
member.param: The kind of the target to which access is provided.

member.summary: Gets the kind of member to which access is provided.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices.UnsafeAccessorAttribute.Name
member.summary: Gets or sets the name of the member to which access is provided.
member.remarks: The name defaults to the annotated method name if not specified. The name must be unset/null for .

member.summary: Specifies the kind of target to which an is providing access.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.ObsoletedOSPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Marks APIs that were obsoleted in a given operating system version.
member.remarks: Primarily used by OS bindings to indicate APIs that should not be used anymore.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.SupportedOSPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Records the operating system (and minimum version) that supports an API. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate support on multiple operating systems.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a or use guards to prevent calls to APIs on unsupported operating systems.
A given platform should only be specified once.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.SupportedOSPlatformGuardAttribute
member.summary: Annotates a custom guard field, property or method with a supported platform name and optional version. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate guard for multiple supported platforms.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a to a field, property or method and use that field, property or method in a conditional or assert statements in order to safely call platform specific APIs.
The type of the field or property should be boolean, the method return type should be boolean in order to be used as platform guard.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.TargetPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Records the platform that the project targeted.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.UnsupportedOSPlatformAttribute
member.summary: Marks APIs that were removed in a given operating system version.
member.remarks: Primarily used by OS bindings to indicate APIs that are only available in earlier versions.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.Versioning.UnsupportedOSPlatformGuardAttribute
member.summary: Annotates the custom guard field, property or method with an unsupported platform name and optional version. Multiple attributes can be applied to indicate guard for multiple unsupported platforms.
member.remarks:
Callers can apply a to a field, property or method and use that field, property or method in a conditional or assert statements as a guard to safely call APIs unsupported on those platforms.
The type of the field or property should be boolean, the method return type should be boolean in order to be used as platform guard.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.CompilerServices2.InlineArrayAttribute
member.summary: Indicates that the instance's storage is sequentially replicated "length" times.

remarks.para: This attribute can be used to annotate a type with a single field. The runtime will replicate that field in the actual type layout as many times as is specified.

member.name: M:System.Runtime.CompilerServices2.InlineArrayAttribute.#ctor(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a new instance with the specified length.
member.param: The number of sequential fields to replicate in the inline array type.

member.name: P:System.Runtime.CompilerServices2.InlineArrayAttribute.Length
member.summary: Gets the number of sequential fields to replicate in the inline array type.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.InteropServices.SuppressGCTransitionAttribute
member.summary: An attribute used to indicate a GC transition should be skipped when making an unmanaged function call.
member.remarks:
This attribute is ignored if applied to a method without the .
Forgoing this transition can yield benefits when the cost of the transition is more than the execution time of the unmanaged function. However, avoiding this transition removes some of the guarantees the runtime provides through a normal P/Invoke. When exiting the managed runtime to enter an unmanaged function the GC must transition from Cooperative mode into Preemptive mode. Full details on these modes can be found at https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/docs/coding-guidelines/clr-code-guide.md#2.1.8. Suppressing the GC transition is an advanced scenario and should not be done without fully understanding potential consequences.
One of these consequences is an impact to Mixed-mode debugging (https://docs.microsoft.com/visualstudio/debugger/how-to-debug-in-mixed-mode). During Mixed-mode debugging, it is not possible to step into or set breakpoints in a P/Invoke that has been marked with this attribute. A workaround is to switch to native debugging and set a breakpoint in the native function. In general, usage of this attribute is not recommended if debugging the P/Invoke is important, for example stepping through the native code or diagnosing an exception thrown from the native code.
The runtime may load the native library for method marked with this attribute in advance before the method is called for the first time. Usage of this attribute is not recommended for platform neutral libraries with conditional platform specific code.
The P/Invoke method that this attribute is applied to must have all of the following properties: * Native function always executes for a trivial amount of time (less than 1 microsecond). * Native function does not perform a blocking syscall (e.g. any type of I/O). * Native function does not call back into the runtime (e.g. Reverse P/Invoke). * Native function does not throw exceptions. * Native function does not manipulate locks or other concurrency primitives.
Consequences of invalid uses of this attribute: * GC starvation. * Immediate runtime termination. * Data corruption.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.InteropServices.UnmanagedCallersOnlyAttribute
member.summary: Any method marked with can be directly called from native code. The function token can be loaded to a local variable using the address-of operator in C# and passed as a callback to a native method.
member.remarks: Methods marked with this attribute have the following restrictions: * Method must be marked "static". * Must not be called from managed code. * Must only have blittable arguments.

member.name: F:System.Runtime.InteropServices.UnmanagedCallersOnlyAttribute.CallConvs
member.summary: Optional. If omitted, the runtime will use the default platform calling convention.
member.remarks: Supplied types must be from the official "System.Runtime.CompilerServices" namespace and be of the form "CallConvXXX".

member.name: F:System.Runtime.InteropServices.UnmanagedCallersOnlyAttribute.EntryPoint
member.summary: Optional. If omitted, no named export is emitted during compilation.

member.name: T:System.Runtime.InteropServices.WasmImportLinkageAttribute
member.summary: Specifies that the P/Invoke marked with this attribute should be linked in as a WASM import.
member.remarks: See https://webassembly.github.io/spec/core/syntax/modules.html#imports.
